title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: April 06, 2026 - Market Reopens After Holiday Weekend

items.headline: U.S. Markets Reopen Monday After Good Friday Closure, Eyeing Jobs Report and Geopolitical Landscape
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are set to reopen today, April 6, 2026, following their closure for the Good Friday holiday on April 3rd. Investors will be keenly reacting to the March Jobs Report, which was released while equity markets were closed. Futures for the S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, and Nasdaq dipped modestly on Friday following the surprisingly strong jobs data. Overall market sentiment leans toward cautious optimism, navigating recent economic data and persistent geopolitical uncertainties.

items.statContext: Closed Thursday, April 2nd, +0.11% for the day. Gained +3.4% for the week ending April 3rd, snapping a five-week losing streak.
Closed Thursday, April 2nd, +0.18% for the day. Led weekly gains with +4.4% for the week ending April 3rd, driven by tech strength.
Closed Thursday, April 2nd, -0.13% for the day. Posted a solid weekly gain of +3.0% for the week ending April 3rd.

sections.title: Top Movers (Monthly Gainers as of April 3, 2026)

items.headline: Franklin Covey Co. Leads Monthly Gainers
items.summary: Franklin Covey Co. ($FC) was the best performing stock in April 2026, as of April 3rd, with a gain of +44.4%.

items.headline: Eastman Kodak Company Sees Significant Rise
items.summary: Eastman Kodak Company ($KODK) showed strong performance, up +20.32% in April 2026 as of the end of last week.

items.headline: Spire Global, Inc. Continues Upward Trend
items.summary: Spire Global, Inc. ($SPIR) was among the top monthly gainers, increasing by +18.91% as of April 3, 2026.

items.headline: March Jobs Report Released on Good Friday
items.summary: The U.S. Labor Department released its March employment report on Good Friday, April 3rd, a day when equity markets were closed. Economists had anticipated 57,000 jobs added in March, a recovery from the previous month's loss, with unemployment holding at 4.4%. The market's reaction to this data is expected to be a significant driver as trading resumes today.

items.headline: Geopolitical Risks and Oil Prices Influence Market Sentiment
items.summary: Geopolitical developments, particularly concerns related to the Iran conflict and rising oil prices, continue to shape market dynamics. Brent crude was trading above $110/barrel, and elevated oil prices are contributing to inflation concerns. This has created a split market, with energy and defensive sectors showing strength, while consumer sectors lag.

items.headline: Major Earnings Reports on the Horizon
items.summary: Looking ahead, several major companies are scheduled to release earnings. Avantor, Inc. ($AVTR) will report its first-quarter 2026 financial results on Wednesday, April 29th. Apple Inc. ($AAPL) is also slated to discuss its second fiscal quarter results on Thursday, April 30th.

items.headline: Sector Performance: Consumer Defensive, Industrials, and Healthcare Lead
items.summary: As of early April 2026, Consumer Defensive, Industrials, and Healthcare emerged as the top-performing sectors. Consumer Defensive averaged a +15% gain, Industrials +8%, and Healthcare +7%. This highlights a rotation towards more resilient sectors amid broader market uncertainties.
